en: On that day they read from the Book of Moses in the hearing of the people. And in it was found written that no Ammonite or Moabite should ever enter the assembly of God,
zh: 那時，他們宣讀摩西的書給眾民聽，見書上寫著說：亞捫人和摩押人永遠不可進入 神的會，

en: for they did not meet the people of Israel with bread and water, but hired Balaam against them to curse them- yet our God turned the curse into a blessing.
zh: 因為他們沒有拿食物和水來迎接以色列人，反而雇用了巴蘭來與他們作對，咒詛他們；但是我的 神使咒詛變為祝福。

en: As soon as the people heard the law, they separated from Israel all those of foreign descent.
zh: 眾民聽見了這律法，就跟一切閒雜人分離。

en: Now before this, Eliashib the priest, who was appointed over the chambers of the house of our God, and who was related to Tobiah,
zh: 這事以前，受委派管理我們 神殿的庫房的以利亞實祭司，與多比雅的關係很密切。

en: prepared for Tobiah a large chamber where they had previously put the grain offering, the frankincense, the vessels, and the tithes of grain, wine, and oil, which were given by commandment to the Levites, singers, and gatekeepers, and the contributions for the priests.
zh: 他為多比雅預備了一間大房間，那裡以前是用來收存素祭、乳香和器皿，還有祭司的舉祭，以及按照命令供給利未人、歌唱者和守門的那些五穀、新酒和新油的十分之一。

en: While this was taking place, I was not in Jerusalem, for in the thirty-second year of Artaxerxes king of Babylon I went to the king. And after some time I asked leave of the king
zh: 這事發生的時候，我不在耶路撒冷；因為在巴比倫王亞達薛西三十二年，我回到王那裡去；過了一段時期，我又向王告了假，

en: and came to Jerusalem, and I then discovered the evil that Eliashib had done for Tobiah, preparing for him a chamber in the courts of the house of God.
zh: 回到耶路撒冷，那時我才知道以利亞實在神殿的院子裡，為多比雅預備一間房間那件惡事。

en: And I was very angry, and I threw all the household furniture of Tobiah out of the chamber.
zh: 我十分惱怒，就把多比雅的一切家具，都從房間裡拋出去。

en: Then I gave orders, and they cleansed the chambers, and I brought back there the vessels of the house of God, with the grain offering and the frankincense.
zh: 又吩咐人潔淨那幾間房間，然後把神殿的器皿，與素祭和乳香搬回那裡。

en: I also found out that the portions of the Levites had not been given to them, so that the Levites and the singers, who did the work, had fled each to his field.
zh: 我發覺利未人應得的分，沒有人供給他們，甚至供職的利未人和歌唱者，各人都跑回自己的田地去。

en: So I confronted the officials and said, "Why is the house of God forsaken?" And I gathered them together and set them in their stations.
zh: 於是我責備眾官長說：“為甚麼疏忽供應 神的殿呢？”我就再召集利未人，使他們重站原來的崗位。

en: Then all Judah brought the tithe of the grain, wine, and oil into the storehouses.
zh: 猶大眾人就把五穀、新酒和新油的十分之一，奉到庫房。

en: And I appointed as treasurers over the storehouses Shelemiah the priest, Zadok the scribe, and Pedaiah of the Levites, and as their assistant Hanan the son of Zaccur, son of Mattaniah, for they were considered reliable, and their duty was to distribute to their brothers.
zh: 我委派示利米雅祭司、經學家撒督和利未人毘大雅作庫官，管理庫房；他們的助手是瑪他尼的孫子、撒刻的兒子哈難；這些人都是忠信可靠的；他們的責任是把人奉獻的各物分給他們的兄弟。

en: Remember me, O my God, concerning this, and do not wipe out my good deeds that I have done for the house of my God and for his service.
zh: “我的 神啊，求你因這事記念我，不要塗抹我為我 神的殿和一切敬拜的禮儀所行的忠誠的事。”

en: In those days I saw in Judah people treading winepresses on the Sabbath, and bringing in heaps of grain and loading them on donkeys, and also wine, grapes, figs, and all kinds of loads, which they brought into Jerusalem on the Sabbath day. And I warned them on the day when they sold food.
zh: 那時，我在猶大看見有人在安息日踹壓酒池，搬運禾捆，馱在驢上，又把酒、葡萄、無花果和各樣的擔子，在安息日運到耶路撒冷；我就在他們賣糧食的那天警戒他們。

en: Tyrians also, who lived in the city, brought in fish and all kinds of goods and sold them on the Sabbath to the people of Judah, in Jerusalem itself!
zh: 又有推羅人住在耶路撒冷城中，他們把魚和各樣的貨物運進來，在安息日賣給猶大人，而且在耶路撒冷販賣。

en: Then I confronted the nobles of Judah and said to them, "What is this evil thing that you are doing, profaning the Sabbath day?
zh: 於是我責備猶大的貴族，對他們說：“你們怎麼行這惡事，褻瀆安息日呢？

en: Did not your fathers act in this way, and did not our God bring all this disaster on us and on this city? Now you are bringing more wrath on Israel by profaning the Sabbath."
zh: 從前你們的列祖不是這樣行，以致我們的 神使這一切災禍臨到我們和這城嗎？現在你們褻瀆安息日，使 神的忿怒越發臨到以色列。”

en: As soon as it began to grow dark at the gates of Jerusalem before the Sabbath, I commanded that the doors should be shut and gave orders that they should not be opened until after the Sabbath. And I stationed some of my servants at the gates, that no load might be brought in on the Sabbath day.
zh: 所以在安息日的前一天，黃昏的陰影臨到耶路撒冷城門的時候，我就吩咐人把城門關閉；我又下令不許開門，直到安息日過了，我又派我的僕人在城門站崗，免得有人在安息日挑擔子進來。

en: Then the merchants and sellers of all kinds of wares lodged outside Jerusalem once or twice.
zh: 於是商人和販賣各樣貨物的，一次兩次在耶路撒冷城外過夜。

en: But I warned them and said to them, "Why do you lodge outside the wall? If you do so again, I will lay hands on you." From that time on they did not come on the Sabbath.
zh: 我警告他們，對他們說：“你們為甚麼要在城牆前面過夜呢？如果你們再這樣，我就要動手對付你們。”從那時起，他們在安息日就不敢再來了。

en: Then I commanded the Levites that they should purify themselves and come and guard the gates, to keep the Sabbath day holy. Remember this also in my favor, O my God, and spare me according to the greatness of your steadfast love.
zh: 我吩咐利未人潔淨自己，然後來看守城門，使安息日分別為聖。“我的 神啊，求你也因這事記念我，照著你豐盛的慈愛憐恤我！”

en: In those days also I saw the Jews who had married women of Ashdod, Ammon, and Moab.
zh: 那些日子，我也發現有些猶大人娶了亞實突、亞捫和摩押的女子為妻。

en: And half of their children spoke the language of Ashdod, and they could not speak the language of Judah, but the language of each people.
zh: 他們的兒女有一半說亞實突話，其他就說他們民族的語言，他們卻不會說猶大話。

en: And I confronted them and cursed them and beat some of them and pulled out their hair. And I made them take oath in the name of God, saying, "You shall not give your daughters to their sons, or take their daughters for your sons or for yourselves.
zh: 我就責備他們，咒詛他們，擊打他們中間的幾個人，拔下他們的頭髮，叫他們指著 神起誓，說：“你們決不可把自己的女兒嫁給他們的兒子，也決不可為兒子或為自己娶他們的女兒。

en: Did not Solomon king of Israel sin on account of such women? Among the many nations there was no king like him, and he was beloved by his God, and God made him king over all Israel. Nevertheless, foreign women made even him to sin.
zh: 以色列王所羅門不是在這些事上犯了罪嗎？在許多國中並沒有一個王能比得上他的；他蒙他的 神所愛， 神立他作全以色列的王，可是連他也被外族的女子引誘犯罪。

en: Shall we then listen to you and do all this great evil and act treacherously against our God by marrying foreign women?"
zh: 難道我們要聽從你們，行這大惡，娶外族的女子為妻，對我們 神不忠嗎？”

en: And one of the sons of Jehoiada, the son of Eliashib the high priest, was the son-in-law of Sanballat the Horonite. Therefore I chased him from me.
zh: 大祭司以利亞實的孫子耶何耶大的一個兒子，是和倫人參巴拉的女婿，我就驅逐他離開我。

en: Remember them, O my God, because they have desecrated the priesthood and the covenant of the priesthood and the Levites.
zh: “我的 神啊，求你記住他們，因為他們玷污了祭司的職分，也污辱了祭司職分和利未人的約。”

en: Thus I cleansed them from everything foreign, and I established the duties of the priests and Levites, each in his work;
zh: 這樣，我就潔淨了他們，使他們脫離一切外族人的污穢，又重新指派祭司和利未人的職責，各人有自己的工作。

en: and I provided for the wood offering at appointed times, and for the firstfruits. Remember me, O my God, for good.
zh: 我又指派人按時供應木柴和奉獻初熟之物。“我的 神啊，求你記念我，施恩給我。”
